text:
*[Bug 19090] New: R CMD build fails on package w/o R/ directory*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19090>

            Bug ID: 19090
           Summary: R CMD build fails on package w/o R/ directory
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: All
                OS: Linux
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Installation
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:csardi.gabor@gmail.com|csardi.gabor@gmail.com>

Create a minimal package:

```
mkdir pkg
echo -e 'Package: test\nVersion: 1.0.0\n' > pkg/DESCRIPTION
touch pkg/NAMESPACE
```

With R release:

```
R-4.6.0 CMD build pkg/
```

```
* checking for file ‘pkg/DESCRIPTION’ ... OK
* preparing ‘test’:
* checking DESCRIPTION meta-information ... OK
* checking for LF line-endings in source and make files and shell scripts
* checking for empty or unneeded directories
* building ‘test_1.0.0.tar.gz’
```

With R-devel:

```
R-devel CMD build pkg/
```

```
* checking for file ‘pkg/DESCRIPTION’ ... OK
Warning in file.create(to[okay]) :
  cannot create file '/tmp/Rtmp7QCrpt/Rbuild17e377a5ddc3/test/DESCRIPTION',
reason 'No such file or directory'
Warning in file.create(to[okay]) :
  cannot create file '/tmp/Rtmp7QCrpt/Rbuild17e377a5ddc3/test/NAMESPACE',
reason 'No such file or directory'
 ERROR
copying to build directory failed
```

Creating an empty `R/` fixes it:
```
mkdir pkg/R
R-devel CMD build pkg/
```

```
* checking for file ‘pkg/DESCRIPTION’ ... OK
* preparing ‘test’:
* checking DESCRIPTION meta-information ... OK
* checking for LF line-endings in source and make files and shell scripts
* checking for empty or unneeded directories
Removed empty directory ‘test/R’
* building ‘test_1.0.0.tar.gz’
```

R-devel build:

```
root@e5161513952a:/tmp/x# R-devel -q -e 'R.version.string'
> R.version.string
[1] "R Under development (unstable) (2026-06-17 r90169)"
```

*[Bug 19087] Security issue with `pdf()`/`postscript()`*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19087>

--- Comment #3 from Elin Waring (<mailto:elin.waring@gmail.com|elin.waring@gmail.com>) ---
Agree validation and/or sanitization is essential and everyone's
responsibility. 

The idea of a toggle is good because it puts the responsibility on the
developer.

Are there any other legitimate uses?  
What about allowing only the specific string `"|cat"` and "|lp" which seem to
be the only two uses found in a search.


<https://github.com/search?q=%2F%5B%5E%5Cw.%5D%28pdf%7Cpostscript%29%5C%28%5Cs*%5B%27%22%5D%5B%7C%5D%2F+-path%3Apostscript.Rd+-path%3Apostscript.R+-path%3Apdf.Rd+-lang%3Ayaml+-lang%3Ahtml+-path%3Asvn-base&type=code>

text:
*[Bug 19091] New: as.numeric on macOS aarch64 rounds "0.9999999999999999" to 1.0 instead of the nearest IEEE 754 double*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19091>

            Bug ID: 19091
           Summary: as.numeric on macOS aarch64 rounds
                    "0.9999999999999999" to 1.0 instead of the nearest
                    IEEE 754 double
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.6.x
          Hardware: Other
                OS: macOS
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Accuracy
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:akitadak@econ.osaka-u.ac.jp|akitadak@econ.osaka-u.ac.jp>

Created attachment 3732
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3732&action=edit>
Minimal R script reproducing the macOS aarch64 as.numeric rounding issue

On R 4.6.0 macOS aarch64 (Apple Silicon, official CRAN arm64 build), the
following decimal-to-double conversion appears to round one ULP too high.

## Minimal example

In R:

    sprintf("%a", as.numeric("0.9999999999999999"))
    # [1] "0x1p+0"

Expected result:

    "0x1.fffffffffffffp-1"

On the same machine, CPython returns the expected value:

    python3 -c 'print(float("0.9999999999999999").hex())'
    # 0x1.fffffffffffffp-1

## Why I believe the expected value is 0x1.fffffffffffffp-1

The two adjacent IEEE 754 doubles around this value are:

    A = 0x1.fffffffffffffp-1  (= 1 - 2^-53)
    B = 0x1p+0                (= 1.0)

Their midpoint is:

    M = 1 - 2^-54
      = 0.999999999999999944488848768742172978818416595458984375

The decimal string "0.9999999999999999" represents the exact rational value:

    9999999999999999 / 10000000000000000
    = 0.999999999999999900000...

This is strictly below the midpoint M, so under IEEE 754 round-to-nearest the
correct result should be A, i.e.:

    0x1.fffffffffffffp-1

not:

    0x1p+0

## Prior discussion

This appears related to the R-help thread "Number changed weirdly when
converting to numeric" from March 2025:

    <https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-help/2025-March/480618.html>

In that thread, a similar 1-ULP issue on macOS arm64 was discussed, with
R_strtod5() in src/main/util.c mentioned as a possible location. I do not claim
this is a new discovery; this report is intended as a formal Bugzilla
escalation with a small reproducer that still reproduces on R 4.6.0.

## Additional observations on the same machine:

    read.csv(text = "x\n0.9999999999999999")$x
    scan(text = "0.9999999999999999", what = numeric(), quiet = TRUE)
    eval(parse(text = "0.9999999999999999"))

all produce 0x1p+0 when printed with sprintf("%a", ...).

A minimal reproducer script is attached. It also prints the R version,
platform, and the exact expected hexadecimal value.

## What I am not claiming

I am not claiming this is a libc problem on macOS. On the same machine, Python
gives the expected value, and I can provide further cross-parser comparison if
useful.

I am not claiming that the exact internal cause is known. The report is only
about the observed behavior of as.numeric() on macOS aarch64.

## Session info

    R version 4.6.0 (2026-04-24)
    Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin23
    Running under: macOS (Apple Silicon)

## Possible duplicates

I could not find a corresponding open Bugzilla report for this specific
macOS aarch64 decimal-to-double parsing case. I am filing this as a formal
Bugzilla report; please mark it as a duplicate if I missed an existing ticket.
